body:
Cherry picked some commits from PR #381 to fix the example test.

One thing that's going to be annoying about testing example scripts is that they evaluate in global scope when including them, so it's going to be easy to get variable name conflicts...

Not sure how to get around this as we agreed not to use functions for examples.

labels.description: Even a perfect program still has bugs
labels.name: bug 🐞

labels.description: Tests get priority in case of emergency evacuation
labels.name: testing 🧪

title: Fix example test

body:
We used docstrings above the `@hascuda` macro to document the `set!` function for GPUs, but on CPUs `@hascuda` evaluates to `nothing` and Julia thinks we're trying to override the docstring for `nothing`.

```
┌ Warning: Replacing docs for `Core.nothing :: Union{}` in module `Oceananigans`
└ @ Base.Docs docs/Docs.jl:223
┌ Warning: Replacing docs for `Core.nothing :: Union{}` in module `Oceananigans`
└ @ Base.Docs docs/Docs.jl:223
┌ Warning: Replacing docs for `Core.nothing :: Union{}` in module `Oceananigans`
└ @ Base.Docs docs/Docs.jl:223
```

Since the main `set!` function is public and documented, these backend GPU `set!` functions can live with comments instead of docstrings.

These warnings would show up whenever someone imported or ran `using Oceananigans` so they were very public and we should get rid of them.

labels.description: Even a perfect program still has bugs
labels.name: bug 🐞

title: Get rid of docs replacement warnings on CPU
